title: Podman 4.0.2 System Service and Docker Compose unexpected EOF with JournalD
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am running podman 4.0.2 with docker-compose v2.2.3 on a custom ARMv7 Linux 5.10 machine with SystemD 249.7+. I have a simple docker-compose.yml file with 1 container running a very noisy 'lumberjack.py' Python script.

The script runs for ~10 hours just fine with journalctl displaying the extremely verbose logs. This initial time to failure seems to depend on the amount of verbosity that the script dumps to the journald log. After that time, docker-compose stops suddenly within several minutes with **EOF** and is continually restarted with SystemD over and over again. The podman system service exits with several errors show below.  Each failure from docker-compose's perspective is this unexpected EOF. Each error from the podman system service's perspective is this error 'broken pipe'. Restarting the podman system service does not fix it and running docker-compose manually does not fix it. When I turn on debugging in the podman system service, the error seems to be related to journald with **failed to get cursor: cannot assign requested address:**

Podman system service debug output:

```
DEBU[0406] Read fd(2) 4096/8193 bytes for container ba9a6ab1e45940a3fb67995358fd7812eee0ed81782cdbf0921a860ff5e2ae59 
DEBU[0406] Read fd(2) 4096/8193 bytes for container ba9a6ab1e45940a3fb67995358fd7812eee0ed81782cdbf0921a860ff5e2ae59 
INFO[0408] Request Failed(Internal Server Error): failed to get journal cursor: failed to get cursor: cannot assign requested address 
http: superfluous response.WriteHeader call from github.com/gorilla/handlers.(*responseLogger).WriteHeader (handlers.go:61)
@ - - [05/Apr/2022:12:56:16 +0000] "GET /v1.40/events?filters=%7B%22label%22%3A%7B%22com.docker.compose.project%3Ddocker-compose%22%3Atrue%7D%7D HTTP/1.1" 500 1224 "" "Docker-Client/unknown-version (linux)"
DEBU[0408] IdleTracker:idle 1m+1h/3t connection(s)       X-Reference-Id=0x40848a0
DEBU[0408] STDOUT/ERR copy completed                    
ERRO[0408] Writing error to container ba9a6ab1e45940a3fb67995358fd7812eee0ed81782cdbf0921a860ff5e2ae59 HTTP attach connection: write unix /run/podman/podman.sock->@: write: broken pipe 
DEBU[0408] STDIN copy completed                         
ERRO[0408] Flushing HTTP buffer for container ba9a6ab1e45940a3fb67995358fd7812eee0ed81782cdbf0921a860ff5e2ae59 HTTP attach connection: write unix /run/podman/podman.sock->@: write: broken pipe 
ERRO[0408] error attaching to container ba9a6ab1e45940a3fb67995358fd7812eee0ed81782cdbf0921a860ff5e2ae59: write unix /run/podman/podman.sock->@: write: broken pipe 
DEBU[0408] Attach for container ba9a6ab1e45940a3fb67995358fd7812eee0ed81782cdbf0921a860ff5e2ae59 completed successfully 
DEBU[0408] IdleTracker:closed 1m+1h/3t connection(s)     X-Reference-Id="%!p(<nil>)"
@ - - [05/Apr/2022:12:56:05 +0000] "POST /v1.40/containers/ba9a6ab1e45940a3fb67995358fd7812eee0ed81782cdbf0921a860ff5e2ae59/attach?stderr=1&stdin=1&stdout=1&stream=1 HTTP/1.1" 200 0 "" "Docker-Client/unknown-version (linux)"
DEBU[0408] IdleTracker:closed 1m+0h/3t connection(s)     X-Reference-Id=0x40848a0
DEBU[0469] API service(s) shutting down, idle for 60s   
DEBU[0469] API service shutdown, 0/3 connection(s)      
DEBU[0469] API service forced shutdown, ignoring timeout Duration 
DEBU[0469] Called service.PersistentPostRunE(/usr/bin/podman --log-level=debug system service --time=60) 
```

Container journald log output form docker-compose:
```

Apr 05 11:42:53 device sh[18873]: lumberjack  | 2022-04-05 11:42:53,071 - 1439627, 
Apr 05 11:42:53 device sh[18873]: EOF
Apr 05 11:42:53 device systemd[1]: device-docker-compose.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
...
Apr 05 11:44:54 device sh[19376]: lumberjack  | 2022-04-05 11:44:52,349 - 1443314, 
Apr 05 11:44:54 device sh[19376]: lumberjack  | 2022-04-05 11:44:52,385 - 1443315, 
Apr 05 11:44:54 device sh[19376]: EOF
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.)  Create a docker-compose.yml file with these contents:

```
version: "2.4"
services:
  app1:
    container_name: "lumberjack"
    hostname: "lumberjack"
    image: "localhost/device-base:1.4"
    init: true
    security_opt:
      - "seccomp=unconfined"
    tty: true
    restart: "always"
    mem_limit: "35m"
    cpus: 0.95
    volumes:
      - "./app1:/app"
      - "./shared_data:/shared_data"
    networks:
      device_internal_net:
        ipv4_address: "10.89.0.2"
    entrypoint:
      - "python3"
      - "/app/lumberjack.py"
    environment: {}
volumes:
  shared_data: null
networks:
  device_internal_net:
    driver: "bridge"
```

2.) Create a **lumberjack.py** script with the following contents:

```
import time
import string
import random
import logging

logging.basicConfig(format='%(asctime)s - %(message).2048s', level=logging.INFO)
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)

count = 0
letters = string.ascii_letters
while True:
    log.info(f"{count}, {''.join(random.choice(letters) for i in range(512))}")
    count += 1
    #time.sleep(random.uniform(0.01, 0.2))
```

3.) Start the docker-compose environment and wait.

**Describe the results you received:**

podman and docker-compose will run my noisy container indefinitely.

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman container started by docker-compose and podman system service fails unexpectedly with **EOF**.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

After a fresh boot, this takes many hours to hit this bug. After the bug is hit once, it seems to happen over and over in a short amount of time ( minutes ).

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
root@device:~# podman --version
podman version 4.0.2

root@device:~# docker-compose --version
Docker Compose version v2.2.3

root@device:~# systemctl --version
systemd 249 (249.7+)
+PAM -AUDIT -SELINUX -APPARMOR +IMA -SMACK -SECCOMP -GCRYPT -GNUTLS -OPENSSL -ACL +BLKID +CURL -ELFUTILS -FIDO2 -IDN2 -IDN -IPTC +KMOD -LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK -PCRE2 -PWQUALITY +P11KIT -QRENCODE -BZIP2 -LZ4 -XZ -ZLIB +ZSTD -XKBCOMMON +UTMP +SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=hybrid

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  os: linux
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Yocto build
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
This also happens with "k8s-file" in the containers.conf file.

**log_driver = "k8s-file"
events_logger = "k8s-file"**

```
Apr 06 12:06:43 device sh[8626]: lumberjack1  | 2022-04-06 12:06:42,226 - 248196, 
Apr 06 12:06:43 banner-fusion-gateway sh[8626]: EOF
```
It doesn't seem to have to do with journals log rotation either. I've been sporadically manually running 'journalctl --rotate' and it seems to keep logging correctly.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@nalind PTAL
Hi - this is still happening. Do you have any suggestions I can try? Thank you.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon PTAL
Hi - I tried the latest common ( v2.1.2  ) and it is still failing. If there is substantial container output, eventually the EOF will occur.
Core error seems to be the journal. I'd imagine it's getting angry about the amount of content we're dumping into it? We've had similar issues elsewhere.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> Core error seems to be the journal. I'd imagine it's getting angry about the amount of content we're dumping into it? We've had similar issues elsewhere.

I hit this condition again with Podman 4.2.0. This time I squelched all output from my container processes. I hit the condition within 1.5 hours of starting my applications, and since then it has been running ~12 hours without the issue.

```
Aug 08 09:10:23 system systemd[1]: Company Docker-Compose Environment was skipped because of a failed condition check (ConditionPathExists=/storage/docker-compose/docker-compose.yml).
Aug 30 22:27:31 system systemd[1]: Started Company Docker-Compose Environment.
Aug 30 22:27:40 system sh[2338]: Network company-internal-net  Creating
Aug 30 22:27:41 system sh[2338]: Network company-internal-net  Created
Aug 30 22:27:41 system sh[2338]: Container data_collector  Creating
Aug 30 22:27:47 system sh[2338]: Container data_collector  Created
Aug 30 22:27:47 system sh[2338]: Container mqtt_client  Creating
Aug 30 22:27:49 system sh[2338]: Container mqtt_client  Created
Aug 30 22:27:49 system sh[2338]: Attaching to data_collector, mqtt_client
Aug 30 23:59:08 system sh[2338]: EOF
Aug 30 23:59:08 system systemd[1]: company-docker-compose.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Aug 30 23:59:08 system systemd[1]: company-docker-compose.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Aug 30 23:59:08 system systemd[1]: company-docker-compose.service: Consumed 4.880s CPU time.
Aug 30 23:59:08 system systemd[1]: company-docker-compose.service: Scheduled restart job, restart counter is at 1.
Aug 30 23:59:09 system systemd[1]: Stopped Company Docker-Compose Environment.
Aug 30 23:59:09 system systemd[1]: company-docker-compose.service: Consumed 4.880s CPU time.
Aug 30 23:59:09 system systemd[1]: Started Company Docker-Compose Environment.
Aug 30 23:59:25 system sh[9719]: Container data_collector  Running
Aug 30 23:59:25 system sh[9719]: Container mqtt_client  Running
Aug 30 23:59:26 system sh[9719]: Attaching to data_collector, mqtt_client
```
So perhaps the frequency of output aggravates the issue but does not cause it 100% of the time?

I bumped docker-compose and conmon to the latest versions and am still seeing this issue:

1.) conmon version 2.1.4
2.) docker-compose v2.10.2
Have you tried to change the logdriver to file?

@rhatdan - yes I have tried changing both of the following to 'file' and 'none'. Unfortunately I still eventually get the EOFs.

```
log_driver = "none"
events_logger = "none"
```

Here is my entire containers.conf file:

```
[containers]
default_capabilities = [
  "CHOWN",
  "DAC_OVERRIDE",
  "FOWNER",
  "FSETID",
  "KILL",
  "NET_BIND_SERVICE",
  "SETFCAP",
  "SETGID",
  "SETPCAP",
  "SETUID",
  "SYS_CHROOT"
]
default_sysctls = [
  "net.ipv4.ping_group_range=0 0",
]
#devices = []
init = true
init_path = "/usr/bin/docker-init"
log_driver = "none"
events_logger = "none"
log_size_max=10485760
log_global_size_max=10485760
[network]
cni_plugin_dirs = ["/usr/libexec/cni"]
default_network = "podman"
default_subnet = "10.88.0.0/16"
network_config_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"
[engine]
env = ["TMPDIR=/container-storage/downloaded"]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
runtime = "crun"
static_dir = "/container-storage/containers/storage/libpod"
stop_timeout = 3
volume_path = "/container-storage/containers/storage/volumes"
```

Another interesting bit of information I found was that if I start the podman system service and then my docker-compose environment, I can always trigger the EOF by restarting the podman system service. I am not sure if that is the same issue ( and maybe a guaranteed way to cause the issue for debugging ). I thought perhaps that if the podman system service goes away, docker-compose would handle that a bit more gracefully since the containers are started and running.
@baude PTAL

Yesterday I changed my SystemD service file to run docker-compose in 'detached' mode:

`ExecStartPre=/bin/sh -c "/usr/bin/docker-compose --ansi never -f ./docker-compose.yml up -d --remove-orphans"`

I still wanted to see the output from each container so I added a 'podman attach' command to the service file as well:

`ExecStartPre=/bin/bash -c "/usr/bin/docker-compose ps | grep -v NAME | tr -s \" \" | cut -d \" \" -f 1 | while read line ; do ( podman attach --no-stdin \"$line\" & ); done"`

Since doing this, it has been running for 17 hours without any EOFs - and journalctl shows my container output.

Without running in detached mode, I would encounter the EOF far before 17 hours. Perhaps the issue is with running docker compose without detached mode against the podman system service for long periods of time.
That is interesting, since the issue could be a resource leak.  If the service stays up for a long period of time, then it becomes more of a daemon, and we could be seeing a conflict happening.

I also had this issue. I started a number of [Archive Warriors](https://wiki.archiveteam.org/index.php?title=Warrior) on two machines. One running a current Fedora with up-to-date packages and a RHEL 9.0 machine. Both systems experienced the error.
I'm currently testing with fewer containers, but will increase the number to try and reproduce the issue.
The problem only appeared after running the warriors, although it then affected other, unrelated containers as well.
I have not yet tried to set "events_logger" to "file" or something else.

I am seeing the same error even with latest podman 4.3.0 and 4.3.1 on fedora 37 arm64:
```
podman run -i ghcr.io/ansible/creator-ee:latest ansible-lint --version
Error: attaching to container fff6dd3fb8c095592bc206c376bd808c5298e3b98eb59961ec0bfc647f43abb0: write /dev/stdout: broken pipe
Exception ignored in: <_io.TextIOWrapper name='<stdout>' mode='w' encoding='utf-8'>
BrokenPipeError: [Errno 32] Broken pipe
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
```

Still the most interesting thing is that this happens only when this command is inside a bash script. If I call the same command from the console, it works.

# Found the cause!
```
podman run ....  | head -n1
```
I assume this is fixed in the latest version
